title: [LowerToHW] Implicit Always Block merging should merge down, not up
body:
We emit a bunch of extraneous wires because LowerToHW is emitting print/stop operations lexically before the condition expressions they use.  For example, in:

```
circuit test :
  module test :
    input clock : Clock
    input a: UInt<1>
    input a2: UInt<1>
    input a3: UInt<1>

    printf(clock, a, "thing")
    node _T_371 = or(a2, a3)
    stop(clock, _T_371, 1)
```

We generate an extraneous `_T` wire:

```
module test(
  input clock, a, a2, a3);

  wire _T;	// x.fir:10:19

  always @(posedge clock) begin	// x.fir:8:5
    `ifndef SYNTHESIS	// x.fir:8:5
      if (`PRINTF_COND_ & a)	// x.fir:8:5
        $fwrite(32'h80000002, "thing");	// x.fir:8:5
      if (`STOP_COND_ & _T)	// x.fir:10:19, :12:5
        $fatal;	// x.fir:12:5
    `endif
  end // always @(posedge)
  assign _T = a2 | a3;	// x.fir:10:19
endmodule
```

This is more egregious in larger modules.

I'll fix this tomorrow.
